texto: You wish you could’ve learned to play piano. You wish you could’ve started drawing when you were young. You wish you could’ve figured out who you wanted to be before you graduated college. You wish you could’ve learned to love yourself sooner. Well you know what? You didn’t. And that’s just something you’re going to have to learn to deal with. But just because you didn’t do it sooner, doesn’t mean you can’t start now.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Daren Colbert que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “You wish you could’ve learned to play piano. You wish you could’ve started drawing when you were …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre motivacao, beginnings, decision-making, com foco em inspiracao e decision-making.
